examples.title: Basic Constructor and Destructor
examples.description: Demonstrates default constructor, parameterized constructor, and destructor for a simple Student class
examples.explanation:
1. Default constructor is called when no arguments are provided
2. Parameterized constructor initializes with custom values
3. Destructor is automatically called when objects go out of scope
4. Destructor has ~ followed by class name and takes no arguments

examples.title: Copy Constructor and this Pointer
examples.description: Demonstrates shallow copy using copy constructor and usage of this pointer
examples.explanation:
1. Parameterized constructor allocates dynamic memory
2. Copy constructor creates a new object as a copy of existing one
3. This example shows shallow copy - both objects share same memory
4. Destructor frees the allocated memory
5. Note: This demonstrates the need for deep copy in real applications

examples.title: Dynamic Memory Management with Constructors/Destructors
examples.description: Demonstrates proper memory management using new/delete with constructors and destructors
examples.explanation:
1. Constructor allocates memory on heap using 'new'
2. Destructor frees memory using 'delete'
3. 'this' pointer holds address of current object
4. Stack objects are automatically destroyed when they go out of scope
5. Heap objects must be manually deleted to avoid memory leaks
6. The destructor is called automatically for stack objects but must be invoked manually for heap objects
